name: Priya Sharma

jurisdictionContext.note: Fields marked OPEN require India bundle to compute correctly

crossBorderContext.nomineeVsBeneficiaryNote: OPEN: In India, pension/EPF nominees are custodians not inheritors — estate planning implication differs from UK. Flagged for India bundle.
crossBorderContext.indianSuccessionActNote: OPEN: Indian Succession Act applies to Indian assets. Will in UK may not cover Indian assets automatically.

epf.nomineeNote: OPEN: nominee is custodian not inheritor under Indian law

nreAccount.note: NRE interest exempt in India; taxable in UK under DTAA Article 11

nroAccount.note: OPEN: 30% TDS withheld in India; DTAA credit claimable in UK self-assessment — not yet claimed

mutualFunds.note: OPEN: Indian CGT rules differ from UK; gains reportable in UK under DTAA

indianLICPolicy.note: OPEN: LIC term plan — nominee in India. UK estate treatment under DTAA is OPEN.

dependants.note: OPEN: cross-border dependency support — India bundle required for full impact

income.overseasIncomeNote: OPEN: NRE interest £28k * 6.5% = ~£1,820 + NRO interest ~£460 + estimated remittance income. DTAA Article 11 applies. Full computation requires India bundle.

openItems.description: Indian LIC policy — UK estate treatment unclear without India bundle. Not counted in protection coverage score.
NRE/NRO/EPF/MF concentration in INR assets creates currency and jurisdiction concentration risk not modelled by UK-only engine.
Indian EPF nominee is custodian not inheritor — dependency exposure scoring for Indian assets cannot be computed without India bundle.
DTAA Article 11 NRO TDS credit not yet claimed in self-assessment. Potential refund of 30% withholding tax on NRO interest.
openItems.resolution: India bundle · flag to user in APQ

openItems.description: Indian mutual fund gains (ELSS + Balanced) reportable in UK under DTAA but not currently declared.
openItems.resolution: India bundle · flag to user in APQ

alerts.headline: No will — Indian and UK assets both unprotected
alerts.context: No will in place. UK intestacy applies to UK assets. Indian Succession Act applies to India assets. A UK will may not cover India assets — two separate instruments may be needed.
alerts.action: Speak to a cross-border estate solicitor

alerts.headline: No life or income protection — mortgage at risk
alerts.context: £380k mortgage, financially dependent parent in India, no protection of any kind. A UK income protection policy does not cover India-based obligations.
alerts.action: Review protection needs — UK and India separately

alerts.headline: DTAA tax credit not claimed — NRO account
alerts.context: India withheld 30% tax on your NRO interest. You can claim a DTAA credit in UK self-assessment — potential refund.
alerts.action: Amend self-assessment or raise with tax adviser

alerts.headline: Old Aviva pension — no beneficiary, high charges
alerts.context: £24k from Infosys UK role. Nomination is 5 years old. Transfer and update nomination.
alerts.action: Transfer to current scheme and renominate

alerts.headline: Higher-rate taxpayer — pension underfunded vs allowance
alerts.context: £95k salary with 40% relief. Current contributions well below £60k annual allowance.

timeline.title: Write UK will
timeline.sub: UK intestacy rules apply today. India assets need separate instrument.

timeline.title: India bundle available
timeline.sub: Full NRE/NRO/EPF/DTAA computation unlocks when India bundle loads

aiSuggestions: Do I need a separate will for my Indian assets and my UK assets?
How does the DTAA between UK and India affect the tax I pay on my NRO account interest?
Should I consolidate my Indian EPF into a UK pension when I have the option?
What UK life insurance amount do I need given my mortgage and my parent in India?

behaviour.risk: Engaged on both sides of the DTAA. Nominations current on your active pension.
behaviour.action: Review the Aviva (Infosys) pension nomination — it's five years old.
behaviour.future: Cross-border users benefit more than most from annual reviews — rules change in two jurisdictions at once.

capital.risk: At roughly £800k total (UK + Indian assets) vs a £1.9m target you're on pace for stage 2.
capital.action: Higher-rate taxpayer — current pension contributions are well below the £60k annual allowance.
capital.future: At 38 with 29 years of compounding ahead, maximising higher-rate pension relief is the single biggest lever.

tax.risk: Higher-rate taxpayer with pension underused. DTAA credit on NRO interest not claimed.
tax.action: Amend self-assessment for the NRO DTAA credit. Increase pension contributions to use 40% relief.
tax.future: Using higher-rate relief during UK residency years is worth more than anything the India bundle will unlock.

protection.risk: No UK life, critical illness or income protection. £380k mortgage plus a financially dependent parent in India.
protection.action: UK life and income protection first. The Indian LIC term plan stays separate.
protection.future: Cross-border dependants need protection in both jurisdictions — UK IPCs don't pay out for India obligations.

cashflow.risk: £20k cash gives about 2.5 months of spending runway. Below the resilience threshold.
cashflow.action: Build emergency fund to 6 months (~£48k) before maxing pension contributions.
cashflow.future: Cross-border income resilience needs extra buffer — DTAA refunds and NRE/NRO flows aren't instant.

debt.risk: £380k mortgage at 4.4% fixed. No student loan, no consumer debt.
debt.action: No debt action needed. Mortgage rate is fine at current pricing.
debt.future: A single well-priced mortgage on a London property is usually the best debt you'll ever take.

estate.risk: No will, no LPA. UK intestacy applies to UK assets; Indian Succession Act applies to India assets.
estate.action: Speak to a cross-border estate solicitor. UK and India likely need separate instruments.
estate.future: Cross-border estates without wills create the most painful probate cases — solved cheaply by acting now.

plans.label: Split UK + India retirement assets by 55
Annual gift to parents in India

scenarios.name: Sell UK BTL before returning to India
